title: portulaca pygmaea
intro: Portulaca pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Portulaca or Dwarf Purslane, is a small, succulent plant belonging to the Portulacaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high content of vitamins and minerals. In herbal preparations, Portulaca pygmaea is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to promote overall well-being.
